Name: numberOfNodeEntries
Description: The number of entries in the node table.

Name: numberOfNodeTraps
Description: The number of traps which were sent to signal status changes of the nodes.

Name: nodeTable
Description: This table shows the contents of node entries. A node is a physical machine hosting one or more containers.

Name: nodeTableEntry
Description: The node table entries.

Name: nodeGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the node.

Name: nodeDisplayName
Description: The nodename.

Name: nodeIpAddress
Description: The ip address of the node.

Name: nodeDescription
Description: A description of the node.

Name: nodeContact
Description: The responsible administrator.

Name: nodeLocation
Description: The location of the node.

Name: nodeStatus
Description: The status of the node. Possible values are:normal, warning, critical.

Name: nodeArchitecture
Description: The architecture of the node, e.g. x86, sunUltra, ...

Name: nodeOsName
Description: The name of the operating system, e.g. Windows, Linux, ...

Name: nodeOsVersion
Description: The version of the operating system.

Name: nodeSnmpAgentVersion
Description: The version of the SNMP agent.

Name: nodeMibVersion
Description: The version of the Management Information Base.

Name: nodeMgmtURL
Description: Points to a page with gives access to the configuration manager of the node if available.

Name: nodeDocURL
Description: Points to the documentation.

Name: nodeIconURL
Description: Points to a file, which describes the location of the shape and the pixmaps for the icons to be used to display the node on the desktop map.

Name: numberOfNodeContainerEntries
Description: The number of containers which are hosted by the nodes.

Name: nodeContainerTable
Description: This table shows the containers which are hosted by the nodes.

Name: nodeContainerTableEntry
Description: The associated containers.

Name: nodeAssocNodeGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the node.

Name: nodeAssocContainerGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the associated container.

Name: nodeAssocContainerDisplayName
Description: The display name of the associated container.

Name: numberOfApplicationEntries
Description: The number of entries in the application table.

Name: numberOfApplicationTraps
Description: The number of traps which were sent to signal status changes of the applications.

Name: applicationTable
Description: This table shows the contents of application entries. Applications use services which are deployed to the nodes.

Name: applicationTableEntry
Description: The application table entries.

Name: applicationGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the application.

Name: applicationDisplayName
Description: The display name of the application.

Name: applicationDescription
Description: A description of the application.

Name: applicationVersion
Description: The version of the application.

Name: applicationContact
Description: The responsible administrator.

Name: applicationStatus
Description: The status of the application. Possible values are:normal, warning, critical.

Name: applicationMgmtURL
Description: Points to an page with gives access to the configuration manager of the component if available.

Name: applicationDocURL
Description: Points to the documentation

Name: applicationIconURL
Description: Points to a file, which describes the location of the shape and the pixmaps for the icons to be used to display the application on the desktop map.

Name: numberOfApplicationServiceEntries
Description: The number of services which are used by the application.

Name: applicationServiceTable
Description: This table shows the services which are used by the applications.

Name: applicationServiceTableEntry
Description: The associated services.

Name: applicationAssocApplicationGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the application.

Name: applicationAssocServiceGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the associated service.

Name: applicationAssocServiceDisplayName
Description: The display name of the associated service.

Name: numberOfContainerEntries
Description: The number of entries in the container table.

Name: numberOfContainerTraps
Description: The number of traps which were sent to signal status changes of the containers.

Name: containerTable
Description: This table shows the contents of container entries. Containers are installed on the nodes. Services are deployed to containers.

Name: containerTableEntry
Description: The container table entries.

Name: containerGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the container.

Name: containerNodeGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the node where the container is installed.

Name: containerDisplayName
Description: The display name of the container.

Name: containerStatus
Description: The status of the container. Possible values are:normal, warning, critical.

Name: containerVersion
Description: The version of the container.

Name: containerMgmtURL
Description: Points to an page with gives access to the configuration manager of the container if available.

Name: containerDocURL
Description: Points to the documentation

Name: containerIconURL
Description: Points to a file, which describes the location of the shape and the pixmaps for the icons to be used to display the container on the desktop map.

Name: numberOfContainerServiceEntries
Description: The number of services which are deployed to the containers.

Name: containerServiceTable
Description: This table shows the services which are deployed to the containers.

Name: containerServiceTableEntry
Description: The associated services.

Name: containerAssocContainerGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the container.

Name: containerAssocServiceGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the associated service.

Name: containerAssocServiceDisplayName
Description: The display name of the associated service.

Name: numberOfServiceEntries
Description: The number of entries in the service table.

Name: numberOfServiceTraps
Description: The number of traps which were sent to signal status changes of the services.

Name: serviceTable
Description: This table shows the contents of a service entry. Services are deployed to containers which are installed on nodes. Services are used by applications. Service-faults can initiate alarm traps which could be send to external faultmanagement systems.

Name: serviceTableEntry
Description: The service table entries.

Name: serviceGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the service.

Name: serviceContainerGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the associated container.

Name: serviceNodeGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the associated node.

Name: serviceDisplayName
Description: The display name of the service.

Name: serviceStatus
Description: The status of the service. Possible values are:normal, warning, critical.

Name: serviceMgmtURL
Description: Points to an page with gives access to the configuration manager of the component if available.

Name: serviceDocURL
Description: Points to the documentation.

Name: serviceIconURL
Description: Points to a file, which describes the location of the shape and the pixmaps for the icons to be used to display the service on the desktop map.

Name: numberOfServiceAlarmEntries
Description: The number of alarm traps which were initiated by specified service-faults.

Name: serviceAlarmTable
Description: This table shows the alarm traps which were generated due to specified faults which appeared in the services.

Name: serviceAlarmTableEntry
Description: The associated alarms.

Name: serviceAssocAlarmState
Description: Status of the associated alarm: alarm status on, alarm status off.

Name: serviceAssocServiceGuid
Description: The unique identifier of the service which has initiated the alarm trap.

Name: serviceAssocAlarmIndex
Description: The alarm table index of the associated alarm, which is defined in the alarm group.

Name: serviceAssocAlarmDescription
Description: A description of the associated alarm.

Name: numberOfAlarmEntries
Description: The number of entries in the alarm-trap table.

Name: numberOfAlarmTraps
Description: The number of alarm-traps which were initiated by specified faults.

Name: alarmTrapTable
Description: This table shows the contents of an alarm trap. Alarm traps are generated if specified faults appear in the services. Objects definitions alarmTimestampOff and alarmTimestampReceived have become obsolete.

Name: alarmTrapTableEntry
Description: The alarm trap table entries.

Name: alarmTableIndex
Description: A consecutive number of the alarm-traps.

Name: alarmId
Description: The unique identifier of the alarm.

Name: alarmServiceGuid
Description: The GUID of the service which raises the alarm.

Name: alarmContainerGuid
Description: The GUID of the container the alarm source is running on.

Name: alarmNodeGuid
Description: The configured (fixed) node id of the node the alarm source is running on.

Name: alarmTitle
Description: The title of the alarm. Possible are any free configurable strings.

Name: alarmState
Description: Status of the alarm:alarm status on, alarm status off. The alarm is still visible if the status changes to 'off'.

Name: alarmSourceGuid
Description: The GUID of the alarm source. Possible values are the configured (fixed) GUIDs of the FaultHandler instance or HP8k trapReceiver instance.

Name: alarmSourceName
Description: The name of the alarm source. Possible values are the configured (changeable) display names of the Fault Handler instance or HP8k trapReceiver instance.

Name: alarmSourceClass
Description: The ClassName of the alarm source. Possible values are the configured (fixed) implementation class names of Fault Handler instance or HP8k trapReceiver instance.

Name: alarmTimestampOn
Description: Date and time of the alarm event generation. See above for a description on the semantics.

Name: alarmTimestampOff
Description: Date and time of the alarm event reset. See above for a description on the semantics.

Name: alarmTimestampReceived
Description: Date and time when the alarm event was received by the FaultHandler. See above for a description on the semantics.

Name: alarmPriority
Description: The priority of the alarm. Possible values are: (0) normal, (1) information, (2) warning, (3) minor, (4) major, (5) critical, (6) unknown.

Name: alarmCategory
Description: The category of the alarm. Possible are any free configurable strings.

Name: alarmDescription
Description: The description of the alarm. Possible are any free configurable strings.

Name: nodeStatusChange
Description: If the status of an node changes, a trap will be send to the management station containing the application identifier and the node status.

Name: applicationStatusChange
Description: If the status of an application changes, a trap will be send to the management station containing the application identifier and the application status.

Name: containerStatusChange
Description: If the status of a container changes, a trap will be send to the management station containing the container identifier and the container status.

Name: serviceStatusChange
Description: If the status of a service changes, a trap will be send to the management station containing the service identifier and the service status. Components are treated like services.

Name: alarmTrap
Description: If an error occurs, there will be checked if the errorClass is configured to trigger an immediate signalling. If so, a trap will be send to the management station containing the information shown above.

Name: resetAlarmTrap
Description: The described alarm to be reset. The alarm has to be deleted.

Name: heartBeatTrap
Description: Heartbeat trap, fired at periodic intervals.

Name: nodeGroupCompliance
Description: The node group compliance.

Name: applicationGroupCompliance
Description: The application group compliance.

Name: containerGroupCompliance
Description: The container group compliance.

Name: serviceGroupCompliance
Description: The service group compliance.

Name: alarmGroupCompliance
Description: The alarm group compliance.

Name: trapGroupCompliance
Description: The trap group compliance.

Name: compliance
Description: The compliance statement.
